instruction:
Let $x,y,$ and $z$ be real numbers satisfying $y-x-z = xyz.$ The maximum value of the expression
$$$\mathcal{P} = \frac{2}{x^2 + 1} - \frac{2}{y^2 + 1} + \frac{3}{z^2 + 1}$$$
can be written as $\frac{p}{q}$ for coprime positive integers $p$ and $q.$ Find $p+q$ .

Options:
A. 14
B. 19
C. 10
D. 11
E. 12
F. 17
G. 18
H. 13
I. 16
J. 15
